title: Seoul Food Street
summary: Gold-standard curriculum pack for the Seoul food street route, covering script, pronunciation, vocabulary, and grammar for first-order hangouts.

nodes.title: Learn 14 basic consonants
nodes.description: Recognize and name the 14 basic Korean consonants.

nodes.title: Learn 10 basic vowels
nodes.description: Recognize and name the 10 basic Korean vowels.

nodes.title: Read 2-part syllable blocks
nodes.description: Combine consonant + vowel to read basic blocks like 가, 나, 다.

nodes.title: Read menu items
nodes.description: Read food names from the stall menu.

nodes.title: Pronounce all jamo correctly
nodes.description: Accurate pronunciation of all consonants and vowels.

nodes.title: Learn 받침 (batchim) rules
nodes.description: Understand and apply final consonant pronunciation rules.

nodes.title: Pronounce 20 food words
nodes.description: Correctly pronounce common Korean food words from the stall.

nodes.title: Learn 20 food items
nodes.description: Know the meaning and reading of 20 street food items.

nodes.title: Learn taste and texture words
nodes.description: Describe flavors: spicy, sweet, salty, delicious, and more.

nodes.title: Learn numbers for ordering
nodes.description: Count 1-5 with native Korean numbers for ordering.

nodes.title: Learn basic food verbs
nodes.description: Key verbs for ordering and eating.

nodes.title: Master N + 주세요 pattern
nodes.description: Use noun + 주세요 to order food politely.

nodes.title: Learn 을/를 object particle
nodes.description: Use the object marker correctly with food nouns.

nodes.title: Use number + counter patterns
nodes.description: Order using 하나, 두 개, and similar counter patterns.

edges.rationale: Menu reading depends on basic block recognition.
Learner must know consonants before pronouncing them.
Learner must know vowels before pronouncing them.
Food vocabulary follows recognition and pronunciation.
Taste words extend the basic food lexicon.
Number ordering builds on food nouns.
Action vocabulary builds on food nouns.
Courtesy phrases land after core nouns are familiar.
주세요 pattern depends on polite phrase familiarity.
Need food nouns before using noun plus 주세요.
Object particle follows the simpler order phrase.
Counter ordering extends noun plus 주세요.

levels.label: VOCABULARY
levels.description: Do I know what they mean?

scenarios.title: Menu Board Warmup
scenarios.description: Tong walks the learner through the menu board before the first order.

scenarios.title: First Order Hangout
scenarios.description: Order 떡볶이 and a drink at the stall using polite Korean.

missions.title: Read the Stall Menu
missions.description: Read the visible menu without Tong reading it out for you.

missions.title: Say the Order Clearly
missions.description: Pronounce core menu items clearly enough for the stall owner to understand.

missions.title: Understand the Counter Chat
missions.description: Recognize key menu, taste, number, and courtesy vocabulary during the interaction.

missions.title: Place the Full Order
missions.description: Place a complete polite order using counters and the 주세요 frame.

grammarTargets.explanation: Object particle: 을 after consonant-ending nouns, 를 after vowel-ending nouns.

sentenceFrameTargets.explanation: Add 주세요 after a noun to politely ask for something.
Use native Korean numbers plus the counter 개 for general items.
Counter phrases commonly used for ordering quantities.
